findings.description: The callout describes the hyperboloid of one sheet as 'like an hourglass that stays connected', but an hourglass shape describes a hyperboloid of two sheets or a cone; the hyperboloid of one sheet looks like a cooling tower (waisted but connected), not an hourglass — the analogy is misleading and contradicts the correct cooling-tower comparison given in the same sentence.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the hourglass analogy and keep only the cooling-tower description, e.g., 'Think of the shape as a cooling tower at a power plant: connected, with a narrowed waist at z=0.'

findings.description: The display-math block showing the standard-form ellipsoid equation is immediately followed by a paragraph (a0ef2bb0) that explicitly interprets its symbols and geometric meaning, but these two blocks do not share a group_id.
findings.suggested_fix: Assign both block 779fd174 and block a0ef2bb0 the same group_id, e.g., group=g-ellipsoid-example (which a0ef2bb0 already has but 779fd174 does not).

findings.description: The general-form display-math equation is immediately followed by paragraph b46279d1 that introduces it and paragraph 1a83fddb that interprets it, but none of these three blocks share a group_id.
findings.suggested_fix: Assign a shared group_id to blocks b46279d1, 3237f3db, and 1a83fddb so the equation and its interpretation are grouped.
